englishDefinition: Something you can hear with your ears, or to seem in a certain way.
A hot liquid food made by boiling vegetables or meat in water.
Having a sharp taste like a lemon.
The place where something comes from.
The direction opposite to north; the bottom of a map.
Located in or coming from the south.
A bean used in foods, or a salty brown sauce made from those beans.
An empty area, or the area beyond Earth where stars and planets are.
Long thin Italian noodles, often eaten with tomato sauce.
To say words; to use a particular language.
A person who gives a talk, or a device that plays sound.
Different from usual; better or more important.
A formal talk given to a group of people.
How fast something moves; or to move very fast.
To say or write the letters of a word in correct order.
The way letters are put together to form words.
To use money to pay for something, or to use time.
A small creature with eight legs that often spins webs.
The part of a person that is not the body, often connected with feelings.
A tool with a small bowl-shaped end and a handle, used for eating.
A game or activity that needs physical effort and skill.
To open out or move over a wider area.
The season between winter and summer when plants begin to grow.
Pairs of lucky red papers with Chinese writing, hung at New Year.
A shape with four equal straight sides and four right angles.
All the people who work for a company or organization.
A raised area in a theater where actors or singers perform.
One of a set of steps that go from one floor to another.
A small piece of paper with a picture, stuck on a letter to send it.
To put your foot down hard on the ground or on something.
To be on your feet in an upright position.
To rise to your feet from a sitting or lying position.
A level of quality that is accepted as normal or correct.
A bright point of light in the night sky, or a famous performer.
To begin doing or happening.
A place where buses or trains stop, or a TV or radio company.
Paper, pens, and other things used for writing.
To remain in a place or condition.
To stay in front of others, doing better than them.
To not go to sleep at the usual time.
A thick piece of meat, usually beef, cooked by frying or grilling.
To take something that belongs to another person without permission.
Hot water in the form of gas, made by boiling.
A movement made by lifting and putting down the foot.
A long thin piece of wood, often a small branch broken from a tree.
To attach something to another using glue or tape.
Not moving; calm and quiet.
Continuing up to now, or up to the time being talked about.
Not willing to spend or share money.
The body part where food goes after you swallow it.
A pain in the stomach.
A small piece of rock.
To no longer move or continue an activity.
A place where things are sold; a shop.
To put something away to keep it for later use.
Very bad weather with strong wind, rain, or snow.
Having strong winds and rain or snow.
A tale of events, real or imagined; or one floor of a building.
A piece of kitchen equipment used for cooking food.
Going in one direction; not curved or bent.
Unusual or unexpected, sometimes hard to understand.
A person you do not know.
Dried grass used for animal beds, or a thin tube for drinking.
A small soft red fruit with tiny seeds on the outside.
A small narrow river.
A road in a town or city, often with houses and shops on each side.
Worry or pressure caused by problems in life.
To say something firmly to show its importance.
To hit something or someone, or to stop work to ask for changes.
Having a lot of physical power; not weak.
To try very hard to do something difficult.
A person who is learning at a school or college.
To spend time learning about a subject.
Things; a general word for objects or material.
Showing a lack of good thinking; silly.
A particular way of doing something or how something looks.
A topic studied at school, or what something is about.
An underground train system used in cities.
To do what you wanted or planned to do.
Achieving something you have been trying to do.
Having achieved what was wanted or planned.
Used to refer to a kind of person or thing already mentioned.
Happening quickly without warning.
A sweet white or brown substance added to food and drinks.
To offer an idea or plan for someone to think about.
A set of matching clothes, usually a jacket and pants or skirt.
To be right or appropriate for someone or something.
The warmest season of the year, between spring and autumn.
The bright object in the sky that gives Earth heat and light.
The first day of the week, between Saturday and Monday.
Having bright sunshine; not cloudy.
Excellent; very good; or extremely.
A large self-service shop selling food and household items.
An evening meal, usually a light one.
To provide things that someone needs.
To help someone by being there for them or giving aid.
Certain; without doubt.
To ride on the top of ocean waves on a board, or to look at websites.
The top or outside layer of something.
To make someone feel suddenly amazed because of something unexpected.
